title: He lives, the great Redeemer lives
firstLine: He lives, the great Redeemer lives,
publicDomainBasis: Anne Steele (1716–1778), George Hews (1806–1873). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
He lives, the great Redeemer lives,
What joy the blest assurance gives!
And now, before His Father, God,
Pleads the full merits of His blood.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Repeated crimes awake our fears,
And Justice armed with frowns appears;
But in the Savior’s lovely face
Sweet Mercy smiles, and all is peace.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Hence, then, ye black, despairing thoughts;
Above our fears, above our faults,
His mighty intercessions rise,
And guilt recedes, and terror dies.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
In every dark, distressful hour,
When sin and Satan join their power,
Let this dear hope repel the dart,
That Jesus bears us on His heart.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
Great Advocate, almighty Friend!
On Him our humble hopes depend;
Our cause can never, never fail,
For Jesus pleads, and must prevail.
